stories.title: CPSC Alert: Vevor Baby Swings + Zigjoy Sleep Sacks Recalled
stories.posting_window: 8:00 AM MT (X + Facebook) / 5:00 PM MT (X second post)

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: CPSC recall: Vevor baby swings violate the Safe Sleep for Babies Act — incline angle exceeds the legal limit, posing a suffocation risk. Stop use now and check cpsc.gov for refund details. 🚨 #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting
x_posts.posting_time: 8:00 AM MT (X + Facebook) / 5:00 PM MT (X second post)

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: Also recalled this week: Zigjoy baby sleep sacks fail flammability standards — a burn risk. Check your child's sleepwear against cpsc.gov/recalls before bedtime tonight. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting
x_posts.posting_time: 8:00 AM MT (X + Facebook) / 5:00 PM MT (X second post)

facebook.long_form:
```
A quick heads-up for Colorado Springs families with young children: the CPSC has issued recalls on two common infant products this week, and both are worth checking right now.

First, Vevor baby swings are being recalled for violating the Safe Sleep for Babies Act. These swings exceed the regulated incline angle, which means a sleeping infant can slide into a position that blocks their airway. If you own a Vevor baby swing, stop using it immediately — even for brief naps or soothing sessions.

Second, Zigjoy baby sleep sacks have been pulled for failing flammability standards. That's a burn hazard at bedtime, which is exactly when kids are in these for extended stretches. If you have one, take it out of rotation until you can verify it's not part of the recall.

There are several other products flagged in the same window: Tuyedoqe travel bassinets (strangulation and fall risk), Bellabu Bear robes (burn hazard), HiQiLi wintergreen oil (poisoning risk for children), and 4our Kiddies anti-tip furniture straps (tip-over risk). All recall details, eligibility, and refund instructions are at cpsc.gov/recalls.

Do you check cpsc.gov regularly, or do you find out about recalls another way? Curious what works for other families.
```
facebook.image_caption:
#### Image Caption — 8:00 AM MT

```
CPSC recall: Vevor baby swings + Zigjoy sleep sacks. Check cpsc.gov now.
```

stories.title: D11 Spring Break Ends Today — Best COS Picks for the Weekend

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text: D11 spring break ends today — kids back Monday. 🌤️ COS weekend: 46°F high. Good day for Garden of the Gods, a hike at Blodgett, or squeezing in one last outing before school resumes. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
```
D11 spring break officially ends today, and D20 families are wrapping up too. Kids are back in school Monday — which makes this weekend the unofficial last hurrah before routines kick back in. 🌤️

The forecast isn't bad: 46°F high today, partly cloudy, about 20% chance of some light precipitation. Totally doable for outdoor time if you dress the kiddos in layers (very COS spring, right?).

If you're looking for ideas: Garden of the Gods is always free and the red rocks are gorgeous in the low spring light. Starsmore Discovery Center in North Cheyenne Canyon is a good option for families with younger kids. If it does rain, CityROCK has a solid indoor climbing option and kids love it.

The warm, dry spring trend means trails are in decent shape — just watch for muddy sections if you head up toward Cheyenne Mountain State Park or Blodgett Open Space.

How did your family's spring break go? Anything you'd recommend for the final weekend?
```
facebook.image_caption:
#### Image Caption — 10:00 AM MT

```
Spring break ends today. One more weekend, COS. Make it count.
```

stories.title: New Trails Coming to COS: Rock Island Bridge + Pikes Peak Greenway

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text: New this spring: Rock Island Trail gets a 210-ft bridge over Sand Creek. Pikes Peak Greenway adds 0.5 miles of new concrete path. 🏃 Good news for families who bike, run, or stroll with kids in COS. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
```
Good news if your family likes getting outside in Colorado Springs: two notable trail improvements are coming this spring.

The Rock Island Trail is getting a 210-foot bridge over Sand Creek. That bridge fills in a real gap — the Rock Island corridor is a great off-road option for families who bike or run but has had some awkward connection points. This makes it more usable end-to-end.

The Pikes Peak Greenway is adding 0.5 miles of new concrete path as part of its ongoing expansion. The Greenway is already one of the most family-friendly routes in the city — relatively flat, well-maintained, connects several neighborhoods — and more of it is always a win.

There's also good news at Starsmore Visitor Center, where ADA improvements have been made, and the Austin Bluffs Spine Trail is another newer addition to the COS trail network. For longer adventures, Blodgett Open Space has 14 miles of trails with solid views and manageable terrain for older kids.

🏃 Which COS trail does your family use most? Always happy to hear what's working for people.
```
facebook.image_caption:
#### Image Caption — 3:00 PM MT

```
Rock Island Trail: new 210-ft bridge coming. Pikes Peak Greenway: 0.5 miles of new path. COS trail season is looking good.
```

stories.title: AAP Drops the 2-Hour Screen Time Cap — What Changed in 2026

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text: The AAP quietly updated its screen time guidance in 2026. The 2-hour daily cap is gone. New focus: quality of content and context — not clock-watching. Here's what the shift means for your family. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
```
If you've ever felt guilty about letting the 2-hour screen time limit slide on a tough day — the AAP just changed the rules.

In 2026, the American Academy of Pediatrics updated its screen time guidance and removed the longstanding 2-hour daily cap for children over 2. The new framework isn't a free pass for unlimited device time, but it's a meaningful shift in how the AAP wants parents to think about the issue. Instead of tracking the clock, the focus is now on what your kids are watching, how they're engaging with it, and what's happening around it.

The AAP's recommended tool is a "family media plan" — essentially a household agreement about when, where, and what kind of screen time is okay. Think: no phones at dinner, one show before bed, video calls with grandparents are different from passive YouTube scrolling.

This is a more realistic framework for how most families actually live. A 30-minute FaceTime with cousins and an educational video during quiet time are not the same as two hours of unsupervised algorithm-driven content, and the old rule didn't distinguish between them.

Has the 2-hour rule ever felt useful to your family, or has it mostly been a source of stress? Curious how COS parents are actually handling it.
```
facebook.image_caption:
#### Image Caption — 12:00 PM MT

```
AAP 2026: the 2-hour screen time cap is gone. Quality and context matter more than the clock.
```

stories.title: Spring Events Preview: Garden of the Gods Art Festival + Spring Fling

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: Mark your calendar, COS families. 🌸 Garden of the Gods Art Festival in April. Spring Fling: April 10-11. Early Literacy Event for Deaf/HH families: April 17. Spring in Colorado Springs is just getting started. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
```
Spring break is wrapping up, but the Colorado Springs events calendar is just getting started. Here's what's coming in April for families. 🌸

The Garden of the Gods Art Festival is on the way in April — an outdoor arts event in one of the most scenic settings in the state. It's a great one for families who want to get outside and expose kids to local art in a low-pressure environment.

Spring Fling is scheduled for April 10-11. Keep an eye on details as they're confirmed — this is shaping up to be a solid family event right in the middle of spring.

On April 17, there's an Early Literacy Event designed specifically for Deaf and Hard-of-Hearing families. This is a meaningful, inclusive resource for families navigating literacy alongside hearing differences.

Spring in Colorado Springs is a good time to be outside more, and April has plenty to work with. Save these dates if any of them fit your family's schedule.

Which spring event are you most looking forward to — or is there something on your radar we should know about?
```
facebook.image_caption:
#### Image Caption — 8:00 PM MT

```
Spring events are coming to COS. Garden of the Gods Art Festival, Spring Fling (April 10-11), and more.
```
